---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---



library(dplyr)

concrete <- read.csv("C:/Users/Tejashwini Saravanan/Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")

str(concrete)

colSums(is.na(concrete))


head(concrete)




names(concrete) <- c("Cement", "BlastFurnaceSlag", "FlyAsh", "Water",
                     "Superplasticizer", "CoarseAggregate", "FineAggregate",
                     "Age", "CompressiveStrength")

names(concrete)


sum(concrete$Superplasticizer == 0)




hist(concrete$CompressiveStrength,
     main = "Distribution of Compressive Strength",
     xlab = "Compressive Strength (MPa)",
     col = "skyblue", border = "white")



library(corrplot)
cor_matrix <- cor(concrete)
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper", tl.cex = 0.8)



model <- lm(CompressiveStrength ~ ., data = concrete)
summary(model)


concrete$predicted <- predict(model)

plot(concrete$CompressiveStrength, concrete$predicted,
     main = "Actual vs Predicted Compressive Strength",
     xlab = "Actual", ylab = "Predicted", col = "blue", pch = 16)
abline(0, 1, col = "red")


ggplot(pred_data, aes(x = actual, y = predicted)) + ...





plot(concrete$Cement, concrete$CompressiveStrength,
     main = "Cement vs. Compressive Strength",
     xlab = "Cement", ylab = "Strength", col = "darkgreen", pch = 16)



model <- lm(CompressiveStrength ~ ., data = concrete)


library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(corrplot)

concrete <- read.csv("C:/Users/Tejashwini Saravanan/Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")




concrete <- read.csv("C:/Users/Tejashwini Saravanan/Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")

str(concrete)        # Structure of the dataset
summary(concrete)    # Summary statistics for each column
head(concrete)       # View the first 6 rows





colnames(concrete) <- c(
  "Cement", "BlastFurnaceSlag", "FlyAsh", "Water", "Superplasticizer",
  "CoarseAggregate", "FineAggregate", "Age", "CompressiveStrength"
)

str(concrete)





colSums(is.na(concrete))

sum(concrete$Superplasticizer == 0)





str(concrete)
colSums(is.na(concrete))
 



library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(corrplot)

par(mfrow = c(3, 3))  # Layout: 3 rows, 3 columns
for (col in names(concrete)) {
  hist(concrete[[col]], main = paste("Histogram of", col), col = "skyblue", xlab = col)
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))  # Reset layout

cor_matrix <- cor(concrete)
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper", tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45)


ggplot(melt(concrete, id.vars = "CompressiveStrength"), 
       aes(value, CompressiveStrength)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Compressive Strength vs. Other Variables",
       x = "Input Variable Value", y = "Compressive Strength")




model <- lm(CompressiveStrength ~ ., data = concrete)

summary(model)


concrete$predicted <- predict(model)
plot(concrete$CompressiveStrength, concrete$predicted,
     main = "Actual vs Predicted Compressive Strength",
     xlab = "Actual", ylab = "Predicted", col = "blue", pch = 16)
abline(0, 1, col = "red")

summary(model)
















concrete <- read.csv("C:/Users/Tejashwini Saravanan/Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")


str(concrete)        
summary(concrete)    
head(concrete)       


colnames(concrete) <- c(
  "Cement", "BlastFurnaceSlag", "FlyAsh", "Water", "Superplasticizer",
  "CoarseAggregate", "FineAggregate", "Age", "CompressiveStrength"
)


str(concrete)



colSums(is.na(concrete))

sum(concrete$Superplasticizer == 0)



library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(corrplot)


par(mfrow = c(3, 3))  # Layout: 3 rows, 3 columns
for (col in names(concrete)) {
  hist(concrete[[col]], main = paste("Histogram of", col), col = "skyblue", xlab = col)
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))  # Reset layout


cor_matrix <- cor(concrete)
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper", tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45)


ggplot(melt(concrete, id.vars = "CompressiveStrength"), 
       aes(value, CompressiveStrength)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Compressive Strength vs. Other Variables",
       x = "Input Variable Value", y = "Compressive Strength")
       


model <- lm(CompressiveStrength ~ ., data = concrete)


summary(model)


concrete$predicted <- predict(model)
plot(concrete$CompressiveStrength, concrete$predicted,
     main = "Actual vs Predicted Compressive Strength",
     xlab = "Actual", ylab = "Predicted", col = "blue", pch = 16)
abline(0, 1, col = "red")
  
summary(model)


install.packages("shiny")

install.packages("packagename")



library(shiny)


concrete <- read.csv("C:/Users/Tejashwini Saravanan/Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")


colnames(concrete) <- c("Cement", "BlastFurnaceSlag", "FlyAsh", "Water", "Superplasticizer",
                        "CoarseAggregate", "FineAggregate", "Age", "CompressiveStrength")

model <- lm(CompressiveStrength ~ ., data = concrete)
concrete$predicted <- predict(model)


ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Concrete Strength Dashboard"),
  
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      helpText("Visualize and Analyze Concrete Strength Data")
    ),
    
    mainPanel(
      tabsetPanel(
        tabPanel("Histogram - Cement", 
                 plotOutput("cementHist")),
        
        tabPanel("Actual vs Predicted", 
                 plotOutput("predictedPlot")),
        
        tabPanel("Model Summary", 
                 verbatimTextOutput("modelSummary"))
      )
    )
  )
)


server <- function(input, output) {
  
  output$cementHist <- renderPlot({
    hist(concrete$Cement, main = "Histogram of Cement Quantity", 
         xlab = "Cement (kg/m^3)", col = "skyblue", border = "white")
  })
  
  output$predictedPlot <- renderPlot({
    plot(concrete$CompressiveStrength, concrete$predicted,
         main = "Actual vs Predicted Compressive Strength",
         xlab = "Actual Strength (MPa)", ylab = "Predicted Strength (MPa)",
         col = "blue", pch = 16)
    abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
  })
  
  output$modelSummary <- renderPrint({
    summary(model)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)


install.packages(c("ggplot2", "plotly", "dplyr", "shinythemes"))

ui <- fluidPage(
  theme = shinythemes::shinytheme("flatly"),  # Try 'cerulean', 'cosmo', 'slate' too
  titlePanel("Concrete Compressive Strength Dashboard"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      sliderInput("age", "Age (days):", min = 1, max = 365, value = 28),
      sliderInput("cement", "Cement (kg/m³):", min = 100, max = 600, value = 300)
    ),
    mainPanel(
      plotlyOutput("scatterPlot"),
      plotlyOutput("correlationHeatmap")
    )
  )
)



server <- function(input, output) {
  # 1. Scatter plot: Cement vs Strength, color by Age
  output$scatterPlot <- renderPlotly({
    p <- ggplot(concrete, aes(x = Cement, y = CompressiveStrength, color = Age)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
      labs(title = "Cement vs Compressive Strength", x = "Cement (kg/m³)", y = "Strength (MPa)") +
      theme_minimal()
    ggplotly(p)
  })

  # 2. Correlation heatmap
  output$correlationHeatmap <- renderPlotly({
    numeric_vars <- concrete %>% select_if(is.numeric)
    corr_matrix <- round(cor(numeric_vars), 2)
    heatmap <- plot_ly(
      x = colnames(corr_matrix),
      y = rownames(corr_matrix),
      z = corr_matrix,
      type = "heatmap",
      colorscale = "Viridis"
    )
    heatmap
  })
}


install.packages(c("shiny", "ggplot2", "plotly", "dplyr", "shinythemes"))


library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(shinythemes)

concrete <- read.csv("Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")


ui <- fluidPage(
  theme = shinythemes::shinytheme("flatly"),  # Try: "cerulean", "slate", "cosmo", etc.
  titlePanel("Concrete Compressive Strength Dashboard"),

  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      sliderInput("age", "Filter by Age (days):",
                  min = min(concrete$Age),
                  max = max(concrete$Age),
                  value = c(28, 365)),
      checkboxInput("showCorr", "Show Correlation Heatmap", value = TRUE)
    ),

    mainPanel(
      plotlyOutput("scatterPlot"),
      br(),
      conditionalPanel(
        condition = "input.showCorr == true",
        plotlyOutput("correlationHeatmap")
      )
    )
  )
)


server <- function(input, output) {

  filtered_data <- reactive({
    concrete %>% filter(Age >= input$age[1], Age <= input$age[2])
  })

  # 1. Cement vs Strength Scatter Plot
  output$scatterPlot <- renderPlotly({
    p <- ggplot(filtered_data(), aes(x = Cement, y = CompressiveStrength, color = Age)) +
      geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
      labs(title = "Cement vs Compressive Strength",
           x = "Cement (kg/m³)",
           y = "Compressive Strength (MPa)") +
      theme_minimal()
    ggplotly(p)
  })

  # 2. Correlation Heatmap
  output$correlationHeatmap <- renderPlotly({
    numeric_vars <- filtered_data() %>% select_if(is.numeric)
    corr_matrix <- round(cor(numeric_vars), 2)

    plot_ly(
      x = colnames(corr_matrix),
      y = rownames(corr_matrix),
      z = corr_matrix,
      type = "heatmap",
      colorscale = "Viridis",
      reversescale = TRUE
    ) %>%
      layout(title = "Correlation Heatmap")
  })
}


shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)



library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(shinythemes)


concrete <- read.csv("Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")

ui <- fluidPage(
  theme = shinytheme("sandstone"),
  titlePanel("Concrete Compressive Strength: Interactive Dashboard"),

  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      sliderInput("ageRange", "Select Age Range (days):",
                  min = min(concrete$Age), max = max(concrete$Age),
                  value = c(28, 365)),
      selectInput("xvar", "X-axis Variable:",
                  choices = names(concrete)[1:8], selected = "Cement"),
      selectInput("yvar", "Y-axis Variable:",
                  choices = names(concrete)[1:8], selected = "CompressiveStrength"),
      checkboxInput("showCorr", "Show Correlation Heatmap", TRUE),
      width = 3
    ),

    mainPanel(
      tabsetPanel(
        tabPanel("Scatter Plot", plotlyOutput("scatterPlot")),
        tabPanel("Box Plot", plotlyOutput("boxPlot")),
        tabPanel("Correlation Heatmap", plotlyOutput("correlationHeatmap"))
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output) {
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    concrete %>%
      filter(Age >= input$ageRange[1], Age <= input$ageRange[2])
  })

  output$scatterPlot <- renderPlotly({
    p <- ggplot(filtered_data(), aes_string(x = input$xvar, y = input$yvar)) +
      geom_point(aes(color = Age), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
      labs(title = paste(input$yvar, "vs", input$xvar),
           x = input$xvar, y = input$yvar) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
    ggplotly(p)
  })

  output$boxPlot <- renderPlotly({
    p <- ggplot(filtered_data(), aes_string(x = "factor(Age)", y = input$yvar)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", color = "darkblue") +
      labs(title = paste("Boxplot of", input$yvar, "by Age"),
           x = "Age (days)", y = input$yvar) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
    ggplotly(p)
  })

  output$correlationHeatmap <- renderPlotly({
    req(input$showCorr)
    corr_data <- filtered_data() %>% select_if(is.numeric)
    corr_matrix <- round(cor(corr_data), 2)
    plot_ly(
      x = colnames(corr_matrix),
      y = rownames(corr_matrix),
      z = corr_matrix,
      type = "heatmap",
      colorscale = "YlGnBu"
    ) %>% layout(title = "Correlation Heatmap")
  })
}

shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)


getwd()


theme = shinytheme("flatly")


library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

concrete <- read.csv("Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")


ggplot(concrete, aes(x = Cement, y = CompressiveStrength, color = Age)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "darkred", size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  labs(
    title = "Cement vs Compressive Strength",
    subtitle = "Colored by Age of Concrete",
    x = "Cement (kg/m³)",
    y = "Compressive Strength (MPa)"
  )
  
  ggplotly(last_plot())




















concrete <- read.csv("C:/Users/Tejashwini Saravanan/Copy of Concrete_Data.csv")

str(concrete)        # Structure of the dataset
summary(concrete)    # Summary statistics for each column
head(concrete)       # View the first 6 rows




This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
plot(cars)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
